content.full_text:
October 10, 1991

Scott Hallabrin, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, Ca 95804

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

The San Bernardino County Sheriff’s Department has developed a newsletter in an effort to increase the level of communications with our organization. This newsletter is currently delivered to only our paid employees.

While we do not pay our Reserve Police officers, Citizens Patrol Officers and others working with us they are for all practical purposes employees as they are covered by Worker’s Compensation when performing their duties. Many of our reserve officers are Level I, meaning they take a patrol car out and perform general law enforcement duties independent of a salaried officer. These unpaid members of the Department need to be given the same treatment as our salaried employees. This requires us to maintain the same level of training and the dissemination of information concerning the department.

We are requesting a written opinion in support of the verbal direction received from John Wallace of your office. We believe that even though these individuals are volunteers, they fall within the scope of internal communications and thus are exempt from Government Code Section 82041.5.

Sincerely,

Dick Williams, Sheriff

By: Paul R. Curry, Sergeant
LEGISLATIVE LIAISON
